Settlement Rules (from docs/SETTLEMENT_RULES.md): - No upfront deposits required - Balances can be negative (represents debt) - Settlement via transferFrom when payer calls settle - Withdrawal of positive balance anytime Security (from docs/SECURITY_BASELINE.md): - ReentrancyGuard on settlement and withdrawals - SafeERC20 for all token transfers - Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern - Atomic settlement (all-or-nothing)","errors":{"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}],"SafeERC20FailedOperation(address)":[{"details":"An operation with an ERC-20 token failed."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"acceptExpense(uint256)":{"details":"Payer cannot accept their own expense. acceptExpense(uint256).details: Payer cannot accept their own expense.      Participant should have approved the contract for their share.      Approval is not checked here - checked at settlement time.

cancelExpense(uint256).details: Only payer can cancel. Only before settlement.

createExpense(uint256,address[],uint256[.details: All participants must be group members.      Payer (msg.sender) can be included in participants.

params.participants: Array of addresses who share the cost
params.shares: Array of share amounts (must sum to amount)

returns.expenseId: The ID of the created expense

settleExpense(uint256).details: Only payer can settle. All non-payer participants must have accepted.      Uses transferFrom to pull each participant's share.      If any transfer fails, entire settlement reverts (atomic). Security: ReentrancyGuard + Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern

withdraw().details: Pull payment pattern. Can withdraw anytime.      ReentrancyGuard protects against reentrancy.

constructor.notice: Creates a new direct settlement group.

settleExpense(uint256).notice: Settle an expense by pulling funds from all participants.

withdraw().notice: Withdraw accumulated balance from settled expenses.
